---
title: "Assessment — Introduction to Machine Learning"
format: html
---

## Questions

Answer the following questions to assess your understanding of the lesson.

### 1. Define Machine Learning

In your own words, provide a concise definition of *machine learning* that captures the idea of learning from experience.

### 2. Identify the two main tasks in Supervised ML

What are the two main tasks in **supervised** machine learning? Name each and briefly describe what the model predicts in each case.

### 3. Identify the two parts of a Data Matrix

In the context of a data matrix used for supervised learning, what are the two main parts (components) we work with? Give their typical mathematical names (e.g., as used in notation).

### 4. Distinguish Features from Target

Explain the difference between **features** and **target** in a supervised learning setting. Which one do we use as input to the model, and which one do we try to predict?

### 5. Merit of using ML

Give one practical reason why we use machine learning instead of hand-crafting rules for every pattern we care about.

### 6. Foundations and related fields

Name one field or discipline that machine learning builds upon or is closely related to, and briefly say how it connects.

### 7. Three main topics of ML

What are the three main topics (or categories) of machine learning typically covered in an introductory course? List them.
